communities.name: AI Tinkerers Geneva
communities.description: New Geneva chapter of the world's largest AI builder community, launching at SDG Solution Space. Follows the AI Tinkerers Lausanne model (400+ engineers engaged at EPFL over 2 years).
communities.whyTarget: Launching May 13, 2026 in Sonar's home city. The chapter launch announcement explicitly names Sonar as part of Geneva's tech ecosystem. No code quality tool currently sponsors any Swiss AI community — founding sponsorship locks in the category and unlocks visibility across the 220+ city global AI Tinkerers network downstream. Time-sensitive.

communities.name: AI Engineer Summit / Code Summit / World's Fair
communities.description: Premier AI engineering conference series. World's Fair (SF, Jun 2025) drew 6,000+ attendees; Code Summit (NYC, Nov 2025) and AI Engineer Europe (London, Apr 2026) cover AI coding, agents, and code quality at depth.
communities.whyTarget: The definitive conference series for AI engineers. AI Engineer Code Summit (Nov 2025, NYC) directly discussed "code review as the ultimate bottleneck" and "AI as a force multiplier that turns weak standards into architectural chaos" — Sonar's exact messaging. Every major AI coding tool competitor is already there; Sonar cannot afford to be absent.

communities.name: AI Tinkerers Global Network
communities.description: World's largest AI builder community with 220+ city chapters and a strict live-code demo format. Tiered sponsorship packages for meetups, VIP dinners, hackathons, and workshops.

Zero code quality competitors currently sponsor any chapter except CodeRabbit at Atlanta.

events.dateRaw: Host an EventBe part of NYC's first official AI Week — a community-led festival featuring dozens of events across the city: host your own panel, talk, demo, workshop, conference or meetup. There is no fee to participate, and the submission process is quick and straightforward. Read our FAQ for info. Anyone is welcome to submit an AI-related event for inclusion in the AI Week NY 2026 program. Approved events will be featured on the official AI Week NY 2026 master calendar (on our website and Luma) and promoted citywide. Submission Deadline: April 20, 2026

HomeAboutHost an EventFAQEvent CalendarAI Week New YorkMay 11-17, 2026Spring Edition • 50+ Events • 10,000+ AttendeesThe city’s premier, community-driven festival by Pulse NYC dedicated to AI (artificial intelligence) — bringing together C-suite leaders, founders, engineers, investors & policymakers across NYC. More Info is coming up!Host an EventAttend EventsSpring Edition (May 11-17)About AI Week New York 2026AI Week New York (Spring Edition) — May 11–17, 2026, organized by Pulse NYC, is a week-long, citywide tech festival featuring a dynamic series of community-led events, interactive workshops, panels, talks, and exclusive networking opportunities. It offers attendees a front-row seat to how artificial intelligence is transforming business, society, and everyday life. Taking place across NYC, AI Week New York 2026 will bring together hundreds of tech leaders, innovators, and practitioners from government, startups, academia, and the broader tech ecosystem to explore the latest breakthroughs in AI, learn from one another, and drive responsible innovation forward — together. The official events calendar is coming up shortly!100+Events20000+Attendees100+Speakers50+PartnersHost an EventBe part of NYC's first official AI Week — a community-led festival featuring dozens of events across the city: host your own panel, talk, demo, workshop, conference or meetup. There is no fee to participate, and the submission process is quick and straightforward. Read our FAQ for info. Anyone is welcome to submit an AI-related event for inclusion in the AI Week NY 2026 program. Approved events will be featured on the official AI Week NY 2026 master calendar (on our website and Luma) and promoted citywide. Submission Deadline: April 20, 20261Finalize DetailsFinalize your event details: date, time, location, title, and description. If you’d like to include AI Week in your event title, please use it only as a tag, formatted as: #AIWeekNY2Create Event PageCreate your Event on Luma (link here) – required for inclusion in the official calendar. Make your event "Public and if you want to control attendance, you can "Restrict Location to Guests" and "Require Approval" on tickets (we can’t include Private events in our calendar as per Luma limitation). 3Add Co-Host & ManagerAdd aiweek@pulse.nyc (Pulse NYC) as a Co-Host and Manager on your Luma event page: both are required for inclusion in the AI Week official calendar! (instructions here)4Include Logo & TextInclude AI Week Logo (creatives here) & the following text anywhere on your Luma event page: This event is part of #AIWeekNY by Pulse NYC — a community-led festival celebrating innovation across the AI ecosystem. More at https://pulse.nyc/ai-week/5. Submit Your EventEvent Tracks & TopicsExplore diverse themes across the weekCreative TechGenerative AI innovations for creative industriesStartup FocusTools and strategies for AI buildersSocial ImpactAI for health, climate, and societal challengesEnterprise ScaleImplementation for large organizationsEthical AIResponsible development frameworksInvestor MatchConnecting startups with capitalAI InfrastructureCloud, compute, data pipelines, and the systems powering modern AIFuture of WorkHow AI is transforming jobs, productivity, and human–machine collaborationEvents CalendarAttend AI Week - Register for EventsWhether you're an AI enthusiast, tech professional, founder, investor, or simply curious about the future of technology, AI Week New York is your chance to be part of something big. Connect with thousands of AI professionals across NYC. Attend cutting-edge talks and hands-on workshops. Explore startup showcases and demos. Network with the brightest minds in tech. Spring Edition (May 11-17)About AI Week New York 2026AI Week New York (Spring Edition) — May 11–17, 2026, organized by Pulse NYC, is a week-long, citywide tech festival featuring a dynamic series of community-led events, interactive workshops, panels, talks, and exclusive networking opportunities. It offers attendees a front-row seat to how artificial intelligence is transforming business, society, and everyday life. Taking place across NYC, AI Week New York 2026 will bring together hundreds of tech leaders, innovators, and practitioners from government, startups, academia, and the broader tech ecosystem to explore the latest breakthroughs in AI, learn from one another, and drive responsible innovation forward — together. The official events calendar is coming up shortly!

events.dateRaw: eMerge Americas is a catalyst, an accelerator, and a global tech conference + expo driving innovative advancements in AI, National Security, Health, and Finance. Join Us in Miami, FL | April 22-24, 2026 eMerge AI + deep tech eMerge NATIONAL SECURITY eMerge HEALTH eMerge FINANCE The Premier Global Tech Conference + Expo The Only Cross-Industry Event for AI + Deep Tech, National Security, Health, and Finance Join Us in Miami, FL | April 22-24, 2026 20 k+ attendees 60 + countries 250 + Speakers 300+ Exhibitors WHY ATTEND? Join the World’s Boldest Thinkers eMerge Americas is a strategic convener and catalyst for innovation, bringing together global enterprises, startups, investors, and government leaders to accelerate advancements in AI + Deep Tech, National Security, Health, and Finance. Through year-round programming and our annual global tech conference + expo in Miami, eMerge Americas connects capital, talent, and ideas to drive impactful progress, strengthen industries, and bring transformative and emerging technologies to market. REGISTER NOW VIEW AGENDA 76% Senior Executives 1,000+ Investors 2,500 + Startups $3B Capital Raised 2023 Highlights This is a headline introducing highlights from last year More highlights Event speakers Meet some of our speakers headline can fit on multiple lines of text More speakers WORLD-CLASS THOUGHT LEADERS 2026 Speakers Emil Michael Under Secretary of War for Research and Engineering Department of War Tim Tebow Entrepreneur, College Football Analyst for ESPN + SEC Network, and 6x New York Times Best-Selling Author Dr. Alex Oshmyansky Co-founder + CEO Mark Cuban Cost Plus Drug Company Jamie Siminoff Founder Ring Col. Nicole Malachowski, USAF (Ret.) First Woman Thunderbird Pilot, Combat Veteran, Fighter Squadron Commander, White House Fellow & Adviser Ali Dasdan Chief Technology Officer Dropbox Manish Goyal VP & Senior Partner, Global AI & Analytics Leader IBM Consulting Nicole Baer Chief Marketing Officer Carta Mariana Atencio Award-Winning Journalist + Author GoLike Media Eric Trump Co-Founder + Chief Strategy Officer American Bitcoin MORE SPEAKERS Powering Innovation Together Partner with us to showcase your brand alongside the forefront of global innovation. eMerge Americas offers influential exposure, networking, and collaboration opportunities. Become a driving force in shaping the future of technology. PARTNER WITH US EXPLORE Dive Into a Unique Experience Step into the forefront of innovation across four dynamic sectors reshaping our future: AI + Deep Tech, Health, National Security, and Finance. Each delivers immersive experiences, cutting-edge tech demonstrations, insightful panels, and headline keynotes, while unlocking unmatched opportunities for cross-industry networking. Choose a path below to explore more. eMerge AI + Deep Tech eMerge NATSEC eMerge HEALTH eMerge FINANCE 2025 HIGHLIGHTS Interact with top tech companies: From founders and powerhouse CEOs to government leaders and today’s top tech minds, eMerge Americas is where bold ideas collide and changemakers across tech, business, and culture connect, create, and ignite what’s next. Meet the Movers & Shakers Spot the Next Unicorn Tech by Day. Miami Vibes by Night. Network with global tech leaders, investors, and innovators 76% of attendees are senior-level executives 60+ countries represented Expanding international pavilions with strong LatAm, Europe and Asia presence #1 region in North America for investment Explore 300+ exhibitors showcasing disruptive solutions Interactive demos from leading companies and startups Hands-on experiences with emerging technologies Cross-industry representation from Fortune 500 to early stage Gain insights from 250+ world-class speakers and keynotes Visionaries from tech, policy, venture capital, and beyond Live Pitch Competition featuring the world’s most promising founders Engage with the eMerge Americas Startup Accelerator + Showcase cohort Explore with cutting-edge content across four high-impact verticals (Health, AI, Finance, and National Security) Programming tailored to industry disruption and collaboration Exclusive year-round content tracks and activations Top thought leaders shaping the future of each sector Connect with startups and VCs in the eMerge Startup Accelerator + Showcase 100+ pre-vetted, high-growth startups 6th in the nation for number of deals closed Curated pitch events, mentor sessions, and investor matchmaking Experience Miami’s vibrant innovation and cultural scene Hosted in the heart of Florida’s scaling tech hub Live music concert, opening night block party, and VIP afterhours events The perfect collision of business, fun, and bold ideas Who Attends eMerge Americas? The eMerge experience is powered by people on the front lines of innovation. From C-suite leaders and startup founders to policymakers, our community represents every corner of the tech ecosystem. Whether you’re here to invest, scale, partner, or learn, this is where unexpected collisions turn into lasting connections. REGISTER NOW “This is the most FUN tech conference I’ve ever been to!” Eric Schmidt Co-founder, Schmidt Futures + Former CEO & Chairman, Google “We’ve met everyone from finance, manufacturing, and everything in between. I’ve been doing this for years and it [eMerge] has by far stood out as being the most productive conference I’ve ever been to.” Steven Gibson Chief Business Officer at Strangeworks “We’ve been involved with this event for three years and it would be hard to find a more mission-aligned organization [eMerge Americas] for what we do and care about, which is around reducing the barriers to entry for entrepreneurship, supporting founders, adding value beyond banking, and really helping to develop thriving innovation ecosystems.” Patrick Johnson VP Growth, Erebor “Here we are, years later, not only talking about all the tech companies that have been very successful coming out of eMerge Americas but we’re also talking about the fact that Miami is the new capital for technology.” Armando Christian Perez, aka Pitbull Grammy-Award® Winning Global Artist, Entrepreneur & Investor Here is a glimpse of our year-round programming eMerge Americas Conference + Expo AI School Generative Gatherings eMpower Community Series Tech Basel Miami AI Summit National Security Demo Day SMB Growth Lab Startup Accelerator + Showcase News + Reports The Latest eMerge News + Insights 2025 Annual Insights Report March 30, 2026 ​​​AI Strategy to Drive Impact by Chief AI Officer Burhan Sebin CTO Magazine March 30, 2026 ​​​Mastercard & Recorded Future as Title Partners for eMerge Americas 2026​​ Mastercard + Recorded Future March 27, 2026 ​​​xTech | Live at eMerge Americas 2026​​ U.S. Army Tech March 24, 2026 VIEW ALL STAY UP TO DATE ON THE MOVEMENT We believe that AI is for everyone and will touch everything—and that Washington state is poised to lead at the intersections of AI and technology, business, art, life sciences, clean energy, civic society, and so much more.AI Week brings together industry experts, creatives, and the broader community to explore the application and real-world value of AI in our businesses, communities, and individual lives—sparking new perspectives, collaborations, and ideas.Mark your calendars for October 27–31, 2025, and join us in recognizing Seattle as a major hub for AI innovation, creativity, and discovery. About #SeattleAIWeekBuilding on last year’s momentum—where 3,500 attendees engaged in over 50 events—we’re expanding even further in 2025! A full-day summit and dozens of community-driven events, there’s something for everyone.Whether you’re a techie, entrepreneur, artist, educator, or simply AI-curious, you’ll find deep conversations, hands-on learning, and valuable networking opportunities all week long.Agenda at a Glance Monday, October 27 Tuesday, October 28 Wednesday, October 29 – Friday, October 31 Opening Party at Seattle AI House. Register Here Full-day AI Summit at Block41 (hosted by WTIA) featuring panels, presentations, and demos from startups. Register Here Community-driven events across the Puget Sound: pitch contests, networking sessions, educational panels, and more.Submit EventBecome a SponsorAI Week is a prime opportunity to reach large audiences through thought leadership and networking.Question? Contact Lexie Rodriguez: lrodriguez@watech.orgCall for Event SubmissionsHave an idea for an event? Want to host a panel, workshop, pitch contest, or happy hour? We want to hear from you. This form gathers interest and details for hosting Seattle AI Week 2025 community events. We’ll use this to build the calendar and help partners coordinate. For example, if two orgs have overlapping ideas, we’ll connect you to potentially collaborate. Contact Lainey Gaffney: lgaffney@watech.org Submit an event Submit your form Email WTIA staff (Lainey Gaffney: lgaffney@watech.org) Wait for the next steps and approval Add event to Luma calendar (instructions provided post-approval)Important DatesDateMilestoneJuly - AugustRegister for Summit + Community Event SubmissionsSeptemberCommunity Event Registration Opens!October 10Last Day to Submit Events to the CalendarOctober 27AI Week begins! 🎉October 28AI Summit at Block41October 29–31Community Events across the regionLet’s Build Something Big! Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) What is Seattle AI Week? Seattle AI Week is a community-powered series of events showcasing the innovation and real-world impact of AI across the Pacific Northwest. From October 27–31, 2025, organizations of all kinds will host panels, workshops, socials, and more—celebrating AI’s role in tech, business, art, science, and beyond. Who can host an event? Anyone! We welcome companies, startups, universities, nonprofits, artists, investors, community orgs, and more. If you’re doing something interesting with (or about) AI, this is your stage. How do I host an event? Submit your idea using this form.Email Lainey Gaffney at WTIA (lgaffney@watech.org) to confirm it was received.We’ll follow up with next steps, including how to get added to the calendar. Are there guidelines for hosting? Yes. Here’s the quick version:Events should be aligned with the spirit of AI Week—community-driven, inclusive, and educational.Free events should be free to attendees.Ticketed events are okay, but pricing should aim to cover costs (not maximize profit).Hosts are responsible for their own logistics (venue, food, speakers, etc.), though we’re happy to connect you with resources.Events must be submitted through the official form—not directly to Luma. Is my event automatically added to the calendar? Nope. We review all submissions to ensure they meet our basic guidelines. Once approved, you’ll receive instructions to add your event to the official Luma calendar. Can I charge for tickets? Yes, but the goal should be cost recovery—not major profit. Think: venue fees, food, materials, etc. Can I host a private or invite-only event? Yes! While we encourage open, public events, we understand some formats (like investor dinners or roundtables) are better suited for a closed audience. Just let us know in your submission form. Can I host a virtual or hybrid event? Absolutely. We encourage in-person events when possible—but we know virtual or hybrid formats can expand access and flexibility. Will you help promote my event? Yes—we’ll list approved events on the official Seattle AI Week calendar, and we’ll highlight select events on social media and email. We also encourage you to promote your event using our logos and social media toolkit (coming soon!). What kind of data do you need from me afterward? We’ll ask for:Total number of registrants and attendeesGeneral breakdown of attendee types (e.g. Tech, Investor, Academia, etc.)We don’t need contact info—just enough to help tell the story of the week’s impact. pastEvents.dateRaw: Mark your calendars! The 7th Edition is scheduled for November 17th and 18th, 2026 in Austin Free Virtual Summit | October 6-7, 2025 Ticketed In-Person Summit | October 8-9, 2025 | Austin Renaissance Hotel 6th Annual MLOps World | GenAI Summit 2025 Get Tickets The event that takes AI/ML & agentic systems from concept to large-scale production 2 Days • 16 Tracks • 75 Sessions • Vibrant Expo Why attend: Optimize & Accelerate Build optimal strategies Learn emerging techniques and approaches shared by leading teams who are actively scaling ML, GenAI, and agents in production. Increase project efficiency Minimize project risks, delays, and missteps by learning from case studies that set new standards for impact, quality, and innovation. Tools for Agent driven apps, multi agent systems, & AI assisted development. Make better decisions Make better, faster decisions with lessons and pro tips from the teams shaping ML, GenAI and Agenetic AI systems in production. Why attend Technical Workshops Workshop: Context Engineering – Practical TechniquesWorkshop: Building AI Agents from ScratchWorkshop: Managing RAGWorkshop: Vibe-Coding Your First LLM AppWorkshop: Conversational Agents with Thread-Level Metrics see full abstracts Industry Case Studies & Roundtable Discussions Case study + Discussion Groups onSLMs + Fine-Tuning: with ArceeSustainable GenAI Systems at TargetExplainable AI at FujitsuAgentic Workforces at Marriot InternationalAgent-Powered Code Migration at Realtor.comand more! Community Event App Meetup 800+ attendees and build your network onsite. See all attendees, connect with speakers. Share your story, build your community. 2 Days of workshops, Case studies, Discussions & Socials Learn from leading minds, sharpen your skills, and connect with innovators driving safe and effective AI in the real world. Free Online Stage Skills Workshops (Oct 6)Expert Sessions (Oct 7) Day 1 Summit:Talks, Panels, & WorkshopsExpo:Lightning TalksBrain DatesCommunity SquareStartup ZoneVendor BoothsOpening Party Day 2 KeynoteSummit:Talks, Panels, & WorkshopsExpo:Lightning TalksBrain DatesCommunity SquareStartup Zone See Speaker abstracts, Workshops & Schedule Why attend: Connect & Grow Grow industry influence Join Brain Dates, Speaker’s Corner, Community Square, or deliver a talk to share your expertise and amplify your industry impact. Equip your team to win Stay ahead of fast-moving competitors by giving your team the insights, skills, and contacts they need to exceed expectations. Build career momentum Make every hour count by using our event app to hyper-focus on the right topics and people who will help shape your future in AI. 2025 Summit: Full-Spectrum AI All themes, talks, and workshops curated by top AI practitioners to deliver real-world value. Explore sessions 2025 THEME: AI Agents & Agentic Workforces AI Agents for Developer Productivity This track highlights practical uses of agents to streamline dev workflows—from debugging and code generation to test automation and CI/CD integration. AI Agents for Model Validation and Deployments Agents can now assist in model testing, monitoring, and rollback decisions. The track focuses on how teams are using autonomous systems to harden their ML deployment workflows. Augmenting Agentic Workforces This track explores how teams are combining human oversight with semi-autonomous agents to scale support, operations, and decision-making across the business. Agents in Production This track explores the design patterns shaping modern agents, from prompt engineering and tool integration to memory and planning strategies, focusing on real-world systems, not just frameworks. It also covers the infrastructure, safety checks, and governance required to deploy agents reliably and securely in production environments, with expert presenters sharing their insights on the challenges of running agents at scale. Latest Trends in MLOps This track covers the key architectural choices and infrastructure strategies behind scaling AI and LLM systems in production, from bare metal to Kubernetes, GPU scheduling to inference optimization. It also addresses the complexities of managing model, data, and pipeline versions in a reproducible, team-friendly way, alongside the unique challenges of deploying ML in regulated, resource-constrained, or air-gapped environments. Expert speakers will share insights on building and operating reliable GenAI and agent platforms at scale while navigating the tradeoffs when cloud-based solutions aren’t an option. 2025 THEME: MLOps & Organizational Scale Governance, Auditability & Model Risk Management This track covers how teams manage AI risk in production—through model governance, audit trails, compliance workflows, and strategies for monitoring model behavior over time. MLOps for Smaller Teams Not every team has a platform squad or unlimited infra budget. This track shares practical approaches to shipping ML with lean teams—covering lightweight tooling, automation shortcuts, and lessons from teams doing more with less. ML Lifecycle Security Security doesn’t end at deployment. This track covers threat models, model hardening, data protection, and supply chain risks across the entire ML lifecycle. ML Training Lifecycle Training isn’t just about epochs and GPUs. Talks focus on reproducibility, retraining triggers, pipeline automation, and how teams manage iterative experimentation at scale. Scoping and Delivering Complex AI Projects This track focuses on scoping and delivering complex AI projects, exploring how teams are adapting their scoping processes to account for LLMs, agents, and evolving project boundaries in fast-moving environments. It also dives into the strategies behind AI product development, from aligning business goals to driving successful delivery and scaling. Expert presenters will share practical insights on navigating the complexities of AI product strategy and execution. 2025 THEME: LLM Infrastructure & Operations LLMs on Kubernetes This track covers the key architectural choices and infra strategies behind scaling AI and LLM systems in production—from bare metal to Kubernetes, GPU scheduling to inference optimization. Learn what it really takes to build and operate reliable GenAI and agent platforms at scale. ML Deployments on Prem This 2025 track covers real-world patterns and pitfalls of running LLMs on Kubernetes. Topics include GPU scheduling, autoscaling, memory isolation, and managing cost and complexity at scale. LLM Observability This 2025 track explores the realities of deploying ML in regulated, resource-constrained, or air-gapped environments. Talks focus on infrastructure design, data access, and managing tradeoffs when the cloud isn’t an option. Data Engineering in an LLM Era What does it mean to observe an LLM in production? This 2025 track unpacks logging, tracing, token-level inspection, and metrics that actually help teams debug and improve deployed models. Inference Optimization & Scaling This track addresses the performance, cost, and reliability challenges of running inference at scale, exploring techniques from token streaming and caching strategies to hardware-aware scheduling. It also delves into low-level optimizations, model compilation, and inference kernels, covering everything from Triton and ONNX to custom CUDA solutions. Expert presenters will share insights into the systems that power fast, efficient, and production-ready AI inference across modern hardware. Multimodal Systems in Production From Triton to ONNX to custom CUDA, this track explores how inference gets faster. Talks focus on low-level optimization, compilation, and maximizing performance on modern hardware. The in-person portion of MLOps World | GenAI Summit takes place October 8-9, 2025 at the Renaissance Austin Hotel.Address: 9721 Arboretum Blvd, Austin, TX 78759, United States See booking details. What’s included with my ticket? Access to all sessions, workshops, networking events, expo hall, and post-event recordings Meals and coffee breaks are provided for in-person attendees. Attendees also have access to the official conference app where you can message speakers, set up brain-dates, attend parties and social functions, post and search for jobs, and see a list of all the other attendees that will be joining in Austin . Attendees will have digital access to over 60k titles and over 200 + other publishers through our official media partnership with O’Reilly publishing Each conference pass will include a 30 days free trial giving you on-demand access to; Live training courses In-depth learning paths Interactive coding environments Certification prep materials Most major AI publications Is there a virtual option? Yes. In the lead-up to the main event, we host 2 bonus virtual days featuring skills training and insights from top AI experts, Oct 6-7th . Learn more What types of sessions can I expect? Technical deep dives, case studies, live demos, hands-on workshops, expert panels, and roundtables across 16+ tracks that have been curated by a volunteer Steering Committee composed of 75+ leading AI practitioners. Also the opportunity to schedule 1-1 brain dates with speakers and other attendees via the app. Are there more active types of experiences? Yes. The expo is where you’ll shift from focused learning to active participating and networking, with Brain Dates, Speakers’ Corner, Community Stage, and Startup Zone. You’ll also find exhibits from companies driving the next wave of GenAI and an opening-night reception to connect with peers. How do I register? You can register from any of the links on our website, including the button in the header. Can I cancel or transfer my ticket? Yes. Tickets are refundable and transferable 30 days prior to the event. See our ticket policy for details Are discounts available for group ticket purchases? Yes. Purchases of multiple tickets receive an additional discount which may vary depending on timing of purchase. Who typically attends MLOps World? AI Engineers, Agentic Developers, Solution Architects, Full-Stack Developers, enterprise AI teams, startup teams, and AI founders. View our About Page to learn more about the event, including the organizing team, Steering Committee, volunteers, and sponsors. Will slides or recordings be available after the event? Yes. The majority of presenters grant permission for their sessions to be recorded and shared. These recordings are made available after the event. The best way to be notified when new learning resources are released is by subscribing to our newsletter. What if I have dietary or accessibility needs? We’ve got you covered. Let us know during registration and we\u2019ll make arrangements."}}]} How do I apply to speak? Submit your proposal via the Call for Speakers link in our site header (available ahead of each event) or subscribe to our newsletter for MLOps and other speaking alerts. Learn more What kinds of talks are accepted? Our Steering Committee reviews technical deep dives, case studies, roadmaps, and skills workshops covering MLOps, GenAI, LLMOps, AI infrastructure, and agentic systems from across the AI spectrum. Are speaker slots paid or unpaid? Speaker slots are unpaid, but all accepted speakers receive a free conference pass and access to networking events. What’s the speaker deadline for slides or submissions? Final slide decks are due 3 weeks before the event. Early drafts may be requested for feedback. Do speakers get free tickets or travel support? Speakers receive a free in-person or virtual pass. We don’t cover travel or lodging, but limited support may be available for nonprofit or academic speakers. Can I speak virtually or only in-person? Both options are available. Some tracks are fully virtual, and remote presentations can be pre-recorded or live-streamed. Are sessions recorded? Will they be shared publicly? Yes. Most sessions are recorded and distributed publicly through our email newsletter, YouTube channel, blog, and social media pages. What kind of A/V support or setup is provided? In-person speakers get full A/V support: mic, projector, and a session moderator. Virtual speakers will receive tech check guidance and support in advance. What are the sponsorship packages and benefits? We offer tiered sponsorship packages that include booth space for lead generation, speaking slots, branding on signage, and digital promotion. Unique package extensions are also available. How do I become a sponsor? Visit our sponsor page to get more details and download our Sponsorship Guide, or contact Faraz Thambi at faraz@mlopsworld.com to discuss availability and options. What is the audience profile? Attendees include ML/Data Engineers, Developers, Solution Architects / Principal Engineers, ML/AI Infra Leads, Technical Leaders, and Senior Leadership (Director, VP, C-suite, Founder) decision-makers from startups, scaleups, and enterprises across North America and around the globe. Can I sponsor the virtual component or a specific track only? Yes. Virtual-only and track-specific sponsorships are available. We also offer branding around keynotes, networking lounges, and workshop zones. How is lead capture handled? With the exception of the Startup Package, all sponsors get lead scanning tools. Virtual sponsors receive opt-in attendee data based on session engagement and resource downloads. Will there be booth space? How big? What’s included? Yes. Booth packages vary in size depending on the tier; they range from a 20’x20’ island booth (Platinum) to a 6’ x 10’ draped booth (Bronze). Please see the guide for full specifications. Can we run our own bespoke event or session? Yes. We offer limited opportunities for sponsor-hosted workshops, roundtables, and after-hours events, pending approval and availability. Are there other ways to get involved as a sponsor? Yes, leading companies can apply to contribute discounts and free trials to our audience of AI/ML practitioners as part of our Stack Drop and Community Code programs. Learn more from our blog or email faraz@mlopsworld.com

The Stage 1 summary report offers an overview of the project so far and a detailed perspective on the opinions of children across Scotland regarding AI and their rights.The Stage 2 report is a summary of the progress and findings of the Stage 2 project and includes a detailed account of the children’s exploration of key themes in relation to children’s rights and AI.Find out more about the project. #block-yui_3_17_2_1_1725362449301_41436 { --tweak-text-block-radius: 0px 0px 0px 0px; communities.description: One of the largest global AI Tinkerers chapters, at Merantix AI Campus. Zed and Vercel are active sponsors.

communities.description: AI Tinkerers Munich has 140+ attendees at monthly events. Air Street is organized by Nathan Benaich (State of AI Report author) for an elite investor/researcher audience.

} } Our Programs. LEARN MORE #block-459a67713ae5b05a35d0 {--sqs-block-content-flex: 0; } Research Workshops at Top AI Conferences LatinX in AI hosts flagship research workshops at premier AI conferences—including NeurIPS, ICML, CVPR, and NAACL—to amplify the voices and contributions of LatinX researchers on the global stage. These workshops provide a welcoming platform for scholars at all stages to present original research, gain visibility, and engage in technical dialogue with the broader AI community.Each workshop features:Peer-reviewed research papers spanning machine learning, computer vision, NLP, ethics, and moreInvited talks from leading LatinX scientists and industry pioneersMentoring sessions, panels, and networking designed to build community and advance careersThrough these workshops, LatinX in AI has built a recognized space for collaboration, visibility, and scholarly excellence—helping hundreds of researchers develop confidence, connect with peers, and access global opportunities in AI. Mentorship That Transforms Careers LatinX in AI’s Mentorship Program connects emerging LatinX talent with experienced researchers, engineers, founders, and industry professionals across the globe. Our goal is to create pathways for growth, confidence, and opportunity in the field of AI.Through structured 1:1 mentorship, group sessions, and peer circles, we support mentees in areas such as:Graduate school applications and academic publishingCareer transitions and technical interviewsResearch feedback and professional developmentWith hundreds of matches made across borders and disciplines, our mentorship network is a cornerstone of our mission—building bridges between generations of LatinX AI leaders and cultivating a more inclusive future for artificial intelligence. Journal of LatinX in AI Research (JLXAIR) The LatinX in AI Research Journal is an open-access publication dedicated to showcasing original work from LatinX researchers across the artificial intelligence landscape. Our journal provides a platform for emerging voices, interdisciplinary ideas, and socially impactful research that often goes underrepresented in mainstream venues.Through regular issues and conference-aligned special editions, we feature:Peer-reviewed papers spanning machine learning, ethics, computer vision, NLP, and moreStudent and early-career research highlightsCollaborative works between academia, industry, and civil societyBy centering LatinX perspectives in AI, our journal not only elevates scholarship but also builds visibility, confidence, and academic credibility for contributors—especially those from developing countries and underfunded institutions. Supercomputer Access Network Through the LXAI Supercomputer Access Network, we provide LatinX researchers, students, and educators with free or low-cost access to cutting-edge AI computing infrastructure—including GPU clusters and supercomputing resources—via partnerships with academic institutions, industry donors, and national labs.This initiative addresses one of the most critical barriers in AI research: lack of access to high-performance computing. Our network enables under-resourced scholars, particularly from Latin America and other developing regions, to:Train large-scale AI modelsRun complex experimentsCollaborate on globally competitive research projectsBy democratizing access to compute, we’re leveling the playing field and fostering a new generation of LatinX innovators in artificial intelligence. LXAI Podcast: Voices of the LatinX AI Community The LatinX in AI Podcast is a storytelling platform that amplifies the voices of LatinX leaders, researchers, entrepreneurs, and changemakers in the field of artificial intelligence. Through in-depth interviews and topical discussions, we explore the intersections of technology, identity, equity, and innovation—bringing forward stories that are too often left out of the AI narrative.Each episode highlights:Breakthrough research and startup journeysInsights into ethical AI, policy, and social impactThe lived experiences of LatinX professionals navigating the tech worldOur podcast builds connection across borders and disciplines while inspiring the next generation of LatinX talent. Whether you’re in academia, industry, or just starting out, the LXAI Podcast offers valuable perspectives from across our global community. LATAM Undergraduate Research Program In collaboration with Google Explore CSR and LatinX in AI, the LATAM Undergraduate Research Program empowers underrepresented undergraduate (and master’s-level) students in computer science and related fields. Through a semester-long mentorship and project-based experience, students develop confidence, critical research skills, and a stronger sense of belonging in the AI community.What participants can expect:Hands-on research projects under the guidance of experienced mentorsOnline workshops, training, and peer activities designed to improve research methods, technical skills, and academic readinessSupport aimed at motivating students to pursue graduate studies in computing and AIThis program not only equips students with practical experience but also strengthens their identity as researchers and accelerates their academic and professional journey in AI. .fe-68c23ca7b0cea560b27e5687 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); } } 1,100+ Grants Awarded Every LatinX in AI research workshop is made possible by generous partnerships with individuals and companies committed to equity in AI. Together, we raise funds for the LXAI Travel and Registration Grant Program, which helps remove financial barriers for underrepresented scholars.Our grants are primarily awarded to students from Latin American countries and Hispanic Serving Institutions in the United States—many of whom would not be able to attend without this support. These grants help cover critical costs like travel, lodging, and conference registration, ensuring that brilliant minds from developing regions can present their work, network, and grow their careers.To date, LatinX in AI has awarded over 1,100 conference and travel grants, directly enabling the participation of LatinX researchers and students in the global AI conversation. Our Impact. 10,000+ Members Served LatinX in AI is proud to serve a vibrant, growing network of over 10,000 LatinX researchers, engineers, entrepreneurs, and students across the globe. Through our targeted programs, we are breaking down systemic barriers and expanding opportunities in the field of artificial intelligence.Our community members benefit from:- Research Workshops at top-tier AI conferences- 1:1 and group mentorship across academia and industry- Collaborative publications via our open-access research journal- Access to AI compute resources for under-resourced researchers and studentsTogether, these programs form a powerful ecosystem for professional growth, academic advancement, and cross-border collaboration—amplifying LatinX voices and talent in AI worldwide. 30+ Countries Represented LatinX in AI is a truly global community, representing all Latin-identified countries across North, Central, and South America, the Caribbean, and beyond. Our members span academic institutions, tech companies, and research labs in over 30 countries, including Brazil, Mexico, Colombia, Argentina, Peru, Chile, Venezuela, the Dominican Republic, and many others.We also proudly serve LatinX individuals across the diaspora—including those based in the United States, Canada, Spain, and the UK—who identify with and support our mission to create a more inclusive AI ecosystem.By uniting voices from across borders, languages, and cultures, LatinX in AI fosters cross-regional collaboration, shared knowledge, and a collective effort to shape the future of artificial intelligence in a way that reflects the diversity of our world. .fe-68c2358ee1f8ff7b36c5c3f0 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); communities.description: Six diversity-focused AI communities: WAI (20,000+ members, 130+ countries, 40+ chapters, June 2025 NA Summit 500+); WiMLDS (~100 chapters, 50,000+ members, 501(c)(3), NYC chapter 4,777 members); Black in AI (5,900+ members, 117 countries, NeurIPS workshop, MacArthur grant); LatinX in AI (NeurIPS/ICRA/ACL workshops); Queer in AI (1,000+ Slack, ACL first archival papers 2025); AI4ALL (Dr. Fei-Fei Li co-founded, Stanford/UCSF summer programs).

} } Our Programs. LEARN MORE #block-459a67713ae5b05a35d0 {--sqs-block-content-flex: 0; } Research Workshops at Top AI Conferences LatinX in AI hosts flagship research workshops at premier AI conferences—including NeurIPS, ICML, CVPR, and NAACL—to amplify the voices and contributions of LatinX researchers on the global stage. These workshops provide a welcoming platform for scholars at all stages to present original research, gain visibility, and engage in technical dialogue with the broader AI community.Each workshop features:Peer-reviewed research papers spanning machine learning, computer vision, NLP, ethics, and moreInvited talks from leading LatinX scientists and industry pioneersMentoring sessions, panels, and networking designed to build community and advance careersThrough these workshops, LatinX in AI has built a recognized space for collaboration, visibility, and scholarly excellence—helping hundreds of researchers develop confidence, connect with peers, and access global opportunities in AI. Mentorship That Transforms Careers LatinX in AI’s Mentorship Program connects emerging LatinX talent with experienced researchers, engineers, founders, and industry professionals across the globe. Our goal is to create pathways for growth, confidence, and opportunity in the field of AI.Through structured 1:1 mentorship, group sessions, and peer circles, we support mentees in areas such as:Graduate school applications and academic publishingCareer transitions and technical interviewsResearch feedback and professional developmentWith hundreds of matches made across borders and disciplines, our mentorship network is a cornerstone of our mission—building bridges between generations of LatinX AI leaders and cultivating a more inclusive future for artificial intelligence. Journal of LatinX in AI Research (JLXAIR) The LatinX in AI Research Journal is an open-access publication dedicated to showcasing original work from LatinX researchers across the artificial intelligence landscape. Our journal provides a platform for emerging voices, interdisciplinary ideas, and socially impactful research that often goes underrepresented in mainstream venues.Through regular issues and conference-aligned special editions, we feature:Peer-reviewed papers spanning machine learning, ethics, computer vision, NLP, and moreStudent and early-career research highlightsCollaborative works between academia, industry, and civil societyBy centering LatinX perspectives in AI, our journal not only elevates scholarship but also builds visibility, confidence, and academic credibility for contributors—especially those from developing countries and underfunded institutions. Supercomputer Access Network Through the LXAI Supercomputer Access Network, we provide LatinX researchers, students, and educators with free or low-cost access to cutting-edge AI computing infrastructure—including GPU clusters and supercomputing resources—via partnerships with academic institutions, industry donors, and national labs.This initiative addresses one of the most critical barriers in AI research: lack of access to high-performance computing. Our network enables under-resourced scholars, particularly from Latin America and other developing regions, to:Train large-scale AI modelsRun complex experimentsCollaborate on globally competitive research projectsBy democratizing access to compute, we’re leveling the playing field and fostering a new generation of LatinX innovators in artificial intelligence. LXAI Podcast: Voices of the LatinX AI Community The LatinX in AI Podcast is a storytelling platform that amplifies the voices of LatinX leaders, researchers, entrepreneurs, and changemakers in the field of artificial intelligence. Through in-depth interviews and topical discussions, we explore the intersections of technology, identity, equity, and innovation—bringing forward stories that are too often left out of the AI narrative.Each episode highlights:Breakthrough research and startup journeysInsights into ethical AI, policy, and social impactThe lived experiences of LatinX professionals navigating the tech worldOur podcast builds connection across borders and disciplines while inspiring the next generation of LatinX talent. Whether you’re in academia, industry, or just starting out, the LXAI Podcast offers valuable perspectives from across our global community. LATAM Undergraduate Research Program In collaboration with Google Explore CSR and LatinX in AI, the LATAM Undergraduate Research Program empowers underrepresented undergraduate (and master’s-level) students in computer science and related fields. Through a semester-long mentorship and project-based experience, students develop confidence, critical research skills, and a stronger sense of belonging in the AI community.What participants can expect:Hands-on research projects under the guidance of experienced mentorsOnline workshops, training, and peer activities designed to improve research methods, technical skills, and academic readinessSupport aimed at motivating students to pursue graduate studies in computing and AIThis program not only equips students with practical experience but also strengthens their identity as researchers and accelerates their academic and professional journey in AI. .fe-68c23ca7b0cea560b27e5687 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); } } 1,100+ Grants Awarded Every LatinX in AI research workshop is made possible by generous partnerships with individuals and companies committed to equity in AI. Together, we raise funds for the LXAI Travel and Registration Grant Program, which helps remove financial barriers for underrepresented scholars.Our grants are primarily awarded to students from Latin American countries and Hispanic Serving Institutions in the United States—many of whom would not be able to attend without this support. These grants help cover critical costs like travel, lodging, and conference registration, ensuring that brilliant minds from developing regions can present their work, network, and grow their careers.To date, LatinX in AI has awarded over 1,100 conference and travel grants, directly enabling the participation of LatinX researchers and students in the global AI conversation. Our Impact. 10,000+ Members Served LatinX in AI is proud to serve a vibrant, growing network of over 10,000 LatinX researchers, engineers, entrepreneurs, and students across the globe. Through our targeted programs, we are breaking down systemic barriers and expanding opportunities in the field of artificial intelligence.Our community members benefit from:- Research Workshops at top-tier AI conferences- 1:1 and group mentorship across academia and industry- Collaborative publications via our open-access research journal- Access to AI compute resources for under-resourced researchers and studentsTogether, these programs form a powerful ecosystem for professional growth, academic advancement, and cross-border collaboration—amplifying LatinX voices and talent in AI worldwide. 30+ Countries Represented LatinX in AI is a truly global community, representing all Latin-identified countries across North, Central, and South America, the Caribbean, and beyond. Our members span academic institutions, tech companies, and research labs in over 30 countries, including Brazil, Mexico, Colombia, Argentina, Peru, Chile, Venezuela, the Dominican Republic, and many others.We also proudly serve LatinX individuals across the diaspora—including those based in the United States, Canada, Spain, and the UK—who identify with and support our mission to create a more inclusive AI ecosystem.By uniting voices from across borders, languages, and cultures, LatinX in AI fosters cross-regional collaboration, shared knowledge, and a collective effort to shape the future of artificial intelligence in a way that reflects the diversity of our world. .fe-68c2358ee1f8ff7b36c5c3f0 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); communities.description: Six diversity-focused AI communities: WAI (20,000+ members, 130+ countries, 40+ chapters, June 2025 NA Summit 500+); WiMLDS (~100 chapters, 50,000+ members, 501(c)(3), NYC chapter 4,777 members); Black in AI (5,900+ members, 117 countries, NeurIPS workshop, MacArthur grant); LatinX in AI (NeurIPS/ICRA/ACL workshops); Queer in AI (1,000+ Slack, ACL first archival papers 2025); AI4ALL (Dr. Fei-Fei Li co-founded, Stanford/UCSF summer programs).

} } Our Programs. LEARN MORE #block-459a67713ae5b05a35d0 {--sqs-block-content-flex: 0; } Research Workshops at Top AI Conferences LatinX in AI hosts flagship research workshops at premier AI conferences—including NeurIPS, ICML, CVPR, and NAACL—to amplify the voices and contributions of LatinX researchers on the global stage. These workshops provide a welcoming platform for scholars at all stages to present original research, gain visibility, and engage in technical dialogue with the broader AI community.Each workshop features:Peer-reviewed research papers spanning machine learning, computer vision, NLP, ethics, and moreInvited talks from leading LatinX scientists and industry pioneersMentoring sessions, panels, and networking designed to build community and advance careersThrough these workshops, LatinX in AI has built a recognized space for collaboration, visibility, and scholarly excellence—helping hundreds of researchers develop confidence, connect with peers, and access global opportunities in AI. Mentorship That Transforms Careers LatinX in AI’s Mentorship Program connects emerging LatinX talent with experienced researchers, engineers, founders, and industry professionals across the globe. Our goal is to create pathways for growth, confidence, and opportunity in the field of AI.Through structured 1:1 mentorship, group sessions, and peer circles, we support mentees in areas such as:Graduate school applications and academic publishingCareer transitions and technical interviewsResearch feedback and professional developmentWith hundreds of matches made across borders and disciplines, our mentorship network is a cornerstone of our mission—building bridges between generations of LatinX AI leaders and cultivating a more inclusive future for artificial intelligence. Journal of LatinX in AI Research (JLXAIR) The LatinX in AI Research Journal is an open-access publication dedicated to showcasing original work from LatinX researchers across the artificial intelligence landscape. Our journal provides a platform for emerging voices, interdisciplinary ideas, and socially impactful research that often goes underrepresented in mainstream venues.Through regular issues and conference-aligned special editions, we feature:Peer-reviewed papers spanning machine learning, ethics, computer vision, NLP, and moreStudent and early-career research highlightsCollaborative works between academia, industry, and civil societyBy centering LatinX perspectives in AI, our journal not only elevates scholarship but also builds visibility, confidence, and academic credibility for contributors—especially those from developing countries and underfunded institutions. Supercomputer Access Network Through the LXAI Supercomputer Access Network, we provide LatinX researchers, students, and educators with free or low-cost access to cutting-edge AI computing infrastructure—including GPU clusters and supercomputing resources—via partnerships with academic institutions, industry donors, and national labs.This initiative addresses one of the most critical barriers in AI research: lack of access to high-performance computing. Our network enables under-resourced scholars, particularly from Latin America and other developing regions, to:Train large-scale AI modelsRun complex experimentsCollaborate on globally competitive research projectsBy democratizing access to compute, we’re leveling the playing field and fostering a new generation of LatinX innovators in artificial intelligence. LXAI Podcast: Voices of the LatinX AI Community The LatinX in AI Podcast is a storytelling platform that amplifies the voices of LatinX leaders, researchers, entrepreneurs, and changemakers in the field of artificial intelligence. Through in-depth interviews and topical discussions, we explore the intersections of technology, identity, equity, and innovation—bringing forward stories that are too often left out of the AI narrative.Each episode highlights:Breakthrough research and startup journeysInsights into ethical AI, policy, and social impactThe lived experiences of LatinX professionals navigating the tech worldOur podcast builds connection across borders and disciplines while inspiring the next generation of LatinX talent. Whether you’re in academia, industry, or just starting out, the LXAI Podcast offers valuable perspectives from across our global community. LATAM Undergraduate Research Program In collaboration with Google Explore CSR and LatinX in AI, the LATAM Undergraduate Research Program empowers underrepresented undergraduate (and master’s-level) students in computer science and related fields. Through a semester-long mentorship and project-based experience, students develop confidence, critical research skills, and a stronger sense of belonging in the AI community.What participants can expect:Hands-on research projects under the guidance of experienced mentorsOnline workshops, training, and peer activities designed to improve research methods, technical skills, and academic readinessSupport aimed at motivating students to pursue graduate studies in computing and AIThis program not only equips students with practical experience but also strengthens their identity as researchers and accelerates their academic and professional journey in AI. .fe-68c23ca7b0cea560b27e5687 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); } } 1,100+ Grants Awarded Every LatinX in AI research workshop is made possible by generous partnerships with individuals and companies committed to equity in AI. Together, we raise funds for the LXAI Travel and Registration Grant Program, which helps remove financial barriers for underrepresented scholars.Our grants are primarily awarded to students from Latin American countries and Hispanic Serving Institutions in the United States—many of whom would not be able to attend without this support. These grants help cover critical costs like travel, lodging, and conference registration, ensuring that brilliant minds from developing regions can present their work, network, and grow their careers.To date, LatinX in AI has awarded over 1,100 conference and travel grants, directly enabling the participation of LatinX researchers and students in the global AI conversation. Our Impact. 10,000+ Members Served LatinX in AI is proud to serve a vibrant, growing network of over 10,000 LatinX researchers, engineers, entrepreneurs, and students across the globe. Through our targeted programs, we are breaking down systemic barriers and expanding opportunities in the field of artificial intelligence.Our community members benefit from:- Research Workshops at top-tier AI conferences- 1:1 and group mentorship across academia and industry- Collaborative publications via our open-access research journal- Access to AI compute resources for under-resourced researchers and studentsTogether, these programs form a powerful ecosystem for professional growth, academic advancement, and cross-border collaboration—amplifying LatinX voices and talent in AI worldwide. 30+ Countries Represented LatinX in AI is a truly global community, representing all Latin-identified countries across North, Central, and South America, the Caribbean, and beyond. Our members span academic institutions, tech companies, and research labs in over 30 countries, including Brazil, Mexico, Colombia, Argentina, Peru, Chile, Venezuela, the Dominican Republic, and many others.We also proudly serve LatinX individuals across the diaspora—including those based in the United States, Canada, Spain, and the UK—who identify with and support our mission to create a more inclusive AI ecosystem.By uniting voices from across borders, languages, and cultures, LatinX in AI fosters cross-regional collaboration, shared knowledge, and a collective effort to shape the future of artificial intelligence in a way that reflects the diversity of our world. .fe-68c2358ee1f8ff7b36c5c3f0 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); communities.description: Six diversity-focused AI communities: WAI (20,000+ members, 130+ countries, 40+ chapters, June 2025 NA Summit 500+); WiMLDS (~100 chapters, 50,000+ members, 501(c)(3), NYC chapter 4,777 members); Black in AI (5,900+ members, 117 countries, NeurIPS workshop, MacArthur grant); LatinX in AI (NeurIPS/ICRA/ACL workshops); Queer in AI (1,000+ Slack, ACL first archival papers 2025); AI4ALL (Dr. Fei-Fei Li co-founded, Stanford/UCSF summer programs).

} } Our Programs. LEARN MORE #block-459a67713ae5b05a35d0 {--sqs-block-content-flex: 0; } Research Workshops at Top AI Conferences LatinX in AI hosts flagship research workshops at premier AI conferences—including NeurIPS, ICML, CVPR, and NAACL—to amplify the voices and contributions of LatinX researchers on the global stage. These workshops provide a welcoming platform for scholars at all stages to present original research, gain visibility, and engage in technical dialogue with the broader AI community.Each workshop features:Peer-reviewed research papers spanning machine learning, computer vision, NLP, ethics, and moreInvited talks from leading LatinX scientists and industry pioneersMentoring sessions, panels, and networking designed to build community and advance careersThrough these workshops, LatinX in AI has built a recognized space for collaboration, visibility, and scholarly excellence—helping hundreds of researchers develop confidence, connect with peers, and access global opportunities in AI. Mentorship That Transforms Careers LatinX in AI’s Mentorship Program connects emerging LatinX talent with experienced researchers, engineers, founders, and industry professionals across the globe. Our goal is to create pathways for growth, confidence, and opportunity in the field of AI.Through structured 1:1 mentorship, group sessions, and peer circles, we support mentees in areas such as:Graduate school applications and academic publishingCareer transitions and technical interviewsResearch feedback and professional developmentWith hundreds of matches made across borders and disciplines, our mentorship network is a cornerstone of our mission—building bridges between generations of LatinX AI leaders and cultivating a more inclusive future for artificial intelligence. Journal of LatinX in AI Research (JLXAIR) The LatinX in AI Research Journal is an open-access publication dedicated to showcasing original work from LatinX researchers across the artificial intelligence landscape. Our journal provides a platform for emerging voices, interdisciplinary ideas, and socially impactful research that often goes underrepresented in mainstream venues.Through regular issues and conference-aligned special editions, we feature:Peer-reviewed papers spanning machine learning, ethics, computer vision, NLP, and moreStudent and early-career research highlightsCollaborative works between academia, industry, and civil societyBy centering LatinX perspectives in AI, our journal not only elevates scholarship but also builds visibility, confidence, and academic credibility for contributors—especially those from developing countries and underfunded institutions. Supercomputer Access Network Through the LXAI Supercomputer Access Network, we provide LatinX researchers, students, and educators with free or low-cost access to cutting-edge AI computing infrastructure—including GPU clusters and supercomputing resources—via partnerships with academic institutions, industry donors, and national labs.This initiative addresses one of the most critical barriers in AI research: lack of access to high-performance computing. Our network enables under-resourced scholars, particularly from Latin America and other developing regions, to:Train large-scale AI modelsRun complex experimentsCollaborate on globally competitive research projectsBy democratizing access to compute, we’re leveling the playing field and fostering a new generation of LatinX innovators in artificial intelligence. LXAI Podcast: Voices of the LatinX AI Community The LatinX in AI Podcast is a storytelling platform that amplifies the voices of LatinX leaders, researchers, entrepreneurs, and changemakers in the field of artificial intelligence. Through in-depth interviews and topical discussions, we explore the intersections of technology, identity, equity, and innovation—bringing forward stories that are too often left out of the AI narrative.Each episode highlights:Breakthrough research and startup journeysInsights into ethical AI, policy, and social impactThe lived experiences of LatinX professionals navigating the tech worldOur podcast builds connection across borders and disciplines while inspiring the next generation of LatinX talent. Whether you’re in academia, industry, or just starting out, the LXAI Podcast offers valuable perspectives from across our global community. LATAM Undergraduate Research Program In collaboration with Google Explore CSR and LatinX in AI, the LATAM Undergraduate Research Program empowers underrepresented undergraduate (and master’s-level) students in computer science and related fields. Through a semester-long mentorship and project-based experience, students develop confidence, critical research skills, and a stronger sense of belonging in the AI community.What participants can expect:Hands-on research projects under the guidance of experienced mentorsOnline workshops, training, and peer activities designed to improve research methods, technical skills, and academic readinessSupport aimed at motivating students to pursue graduate studies in computing and AIThis program not only equips students with practical experience but also strengthens their identity as researchers and accelerates their academic and professional journey in AI. .fe-68c23ca7b0cea560b27e5687 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); } } 1,100+ Grants Awarded Every LatinX in AI research workshop is made possible by generous partnerships with individuals and companies committed to equity in AI. Together, we raise funds for the LXAI Travel and Registration Grant Program, which helps remove financial barriers for underrepresented scholars.Our grants are primarily awarded to students from Latin American countries and Hispanic Serving Institutions in the United States—many of whom would not be able to attend without this support. These grants help cover critical costs like travel, lodging, and conference registration, ensuring that brilliant minds from developing regions can present their work, network, and grow their careers.To date, LatinX in AI has awarded over 1,100 conference and travel grants, directly enabling the participation of LatinX researchers and students in the global AI conversation. Our Impact. 10,000+ Members Served LatinX in AI is proud to serve a vibrant, growing network of over 10,000 LatinX researchers, engineers, entrepreneurs, and students across the globe. Through our targeted programs, we are breaking down systemic barriers and expanding opportunities in the field of artificial intelligence.Our community members benefit from:- Research Workshops at top-tier AI conferences- 1:1 and group mentorship across academia and industry- Collaborative publications via our open-access research journal- Access to AI compute resources for under-resourced researchers and studentsTogether, these programs form a powerful ecosystem for professional growth, academic advancement, and cross-border collaboration—amplifying LatinX voices and talent in AI worldwide. 30+ Countries Represented LatinX in AI is a truly global community, representing all Latin-identified countries across North, Central, and South America, the Caribbean, and beyond. Our members span academic institutions, tech companies, and research labs in over 30 countries, including Brazil, Mexico, Colombia, Argentina, Peru, Chile, Venezuela, the Dominican Republic, and many others.We also proudly serve LatinX individuals across the diaspora—including those based in the United States, Canada, Spain, and the UK—who identify with and support our mission to create a more inclusive AI ecosystem.By uniting voices from across borders, languages, and cultures, LatinX in AI fosters cross-regional collaboration, shared knowledge, and a collective effort to shape the future of artificial intelligence in a way that reflects the diversity of our world. .fe-68c2358ee1f8ff7b36c5c3f0 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); communities.description: Six diversity-focused AI communities: WAI (20,000+ members, 130+ countries, 40+ chapters, June 2025 NA Summit 500+); WiMLDS (~100 chapters, 50,000+ members, 501(c)(3), NYC chapter 4,777 members); Black in AI (5,900+ members, 117 countries, NeurIPS workshop, MacArthur grant); LatinX in AI (NeurIPS/ICRA/ACL workshops); Queer in AI (1,000+ Slack, ACL first archival papers 2025); AI4ALL (Dr. Fei-Fei Li co-founded, Stanford/UCSF summer programs).

} } Our Programs. LEARN MORE #block-459a67713ae5b05a35d0 {--sqs-block-content-flex: 0; } Research Workshops at Top AI Conferences LatinX in AI hosts flagship research workshops at premier AI conferences—including NeurIPS, ICML, CVPR, and NAACL—to amplify the voices and contributions of LatinX researchers on the global stage. These workshops provide a welcoming platform for scholars at all stages to present original research, gain visibility, and engage in technical dialogue with the broader AI community.Each workshop features:Peer-reviewed research papers spanning machine learning, computer vision, NLP, ethics, and moreInvited talks from leading LatinX scientists and industry pioneersMentoring sessions, panels, and networking designed to build community and advance careersThrough these workshops, LatinX in AI has built a recognized space for collaboration, visibility, and scholarly excellence—helping hundreds of researchers develop confidence, connect with peers, and access global opportunities in AI. Mentorship That Transforms Careers LatinX in AI’s Mentorship Program connects emerging LatinX talent with experienced researchers, engineers, founders, and industry professionals across the globe. Our goal is to create pathways for growth, confidence, and opportunity in the field of AI.Through structured 1:1 mentorship, group sessions, and peer circles, we support mentees in areas such as:Graduate school applications and academic publishingCareer transitions and technical interviewsResearch feedback and professional developmentWith hundreds of matches made across borders and disciplines, our mentorship network is a cornerstone of our mission—building bridges between generations of LatinX AI leaders and cultivating a more inclusive future for artificial intelligence. Journal of LatinX in AI Research (JLXAIR) The LatinX in AI Research Journal is an open-access publication dedicated to showcasing original work from LatinX researchers across the artificial intelligence landscape. Our journal provides a platform for emerging voices, interdisciplinary ideas, and socially impactful research that often goes underrepresented in mainstream venues.Through regular issues and conference-aligned special editions, we feature:Peer-reviewed papers spanning machine learning, ethics, computer vision, NLP, and moreStudent and early-career research highlightsCollaborative works between academia, industry, and civil societyBy centering LatinX perspectives in AI, our journal not only elevates scholarship but also builds visibility, confidence, and academic credibility for contributors—especially those from developing countries and underfunded institutions. Supercomputer Access Network Through the LXAI Supercomputer Access Network, we provide LatinX researchers, students, and educators with free or low-cost access to cutting-edge AI computing infrastructure—including GPU clusters and supercomputing resources—via partnerships with academic institutions, industry donors, and national labs.This initiative addresses one of the most critical barriers in AI research: lack of access to high-performance computing. Our network enables under-resourced scholars, particularly from Latin America and other developing regions, to:Train large-scale AI modelsRun complex experimentsCollaborate on globally competitive research projectsBy democratizing access to compute, we’re leveling the playing field and fostering a new generation of LatinX innovators in artificial intelligence. LXAI Podcast: Voices of the LatinX AI Community The LatinX in AI Podcast is a storytelling platform that amplifies the voices of LatinX leaders, researchers, entrepreneurs, and changemakers in the field of artificial intelligence. Through in-depth interviews and topical discussions, we explore the intersections of technology, identity, equity, and innovation—bringing forward stories that are too often left out of the AI narrative.Each episode highlights:Breakthrough research and startup journeysInsights into ethical AI, policy, and social impactThe lived experiences of LatinX professionals navigating the tech worldOur podcast builds connection across borders and disciplines while inspiring the next generation of LatinX talent. Whether you’re in academia, industry, or just starting out, the LXAI Podcast offers valuable perspectives from across our global community. LATAM Undergraduate Research Program In collaboration with Google Explore CSR and LatinX in AI, the LATAM Undergraduate Research Program empowers underrepresented undergraduate (and master’s-level) students in computer science and related fields. Through a semester-long mentorship and project-based experience, students develop confidence, critical research skills, and a stronger sense of belonging in the AI community.What participants can expect:Hands-on research projects under the guidance of experienced mentorsOnline workshops, training, and peer activities designed to improve research methods, technical skills, and academic readinessSupport aimed at motivating students to pursue graduate studies in computing and AIThis program not only equips students with practical experience but also strengthens their identity as researchers and accelerates their academic and professional journey in AI. .fe-68c23ca7b0cea560b27e5687 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); } } 1,100+ Grants Awarded Every LatinX in AI research workshop is made possible by generous partnerships with individuals and companies committed to equity in AI. Together, we raise funds for the LXAI Travel and Registration Grant Program, which helps remove financial barriers for underrepresented scholars.Our grants are primarily awarded to students from Latin American countries and Hispanic Serving Institutions in the United States—many of whom would not be able to attend without this support. These grants help cover critical costs like travel, lodging, and conference registration, ensuring that brilliant minds from developing regions can present their work, network, and grow their careers.To date, LatinX in AI has awarded over 1,100 conference and travel grants, directly enabling the participation of LatinX researchers and students in the global AI conversation. Our Impact. 10,000+ Members Served LatinX in AI is proud to serve a vibrant, growing network of over 10,000 LatinX researchers, engineers, entrepreneurs, and students across the globe. Through our targeted programs, we are breaking down systemic barriers and expanding opportunities in the field of artificial intelligence.Our community members benefit from:- Research Workshops at top-tier AI conferences- 1:1 and group mentorship across academia and industry- Collaborative publications via our open-access research journal- Access to AI compute resources for under-resourced researchers and studentsTogether, these programs form a powerful ecosystem for professional growth, academic advancement, and cross-border collaboration—amplifying LatinX voices and talent in AI worldwide. 30+ Countries Represented LatinX in AI is a truly global community, representing all Latin-identified countries across North, Central, and South America, the Caribbean, and beyond. Our members span academic institutions, tech companies, and research labs in over 30 countries, including Brazil, Mexico, Colombia, Argentina, Peru, Chile, Venezuela, the Dominican Republic, and many others.We also proudly serve LatinX individuals across the diaspora—including those based in the United States, Canada, Spain, and the UK—who identify with and support our mission to create a more inclusive AI ecosystem.By uniting voices from across borders, languages, and cultures, LatinX in AI fosters cross-regional collaboration, shared knowledge, and a collective effort to shape the future of artificial intelligence in a way that reflects the diversity of our world. .fe-68c2358ee1f8ff7b36c5c3f0 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); communities.description: Six diversity-focused AI communities: WAI (20,000+ members, 130+ countries, 40+ chapters, June 2025 NA Summit 500+); WiMLDS (~100 chapters, 50,000+ members, 501(c)(3), NYC chapter 4,777 members); Black in AI (5,900+ members, 117 countries, NeurIPS workshop, MacArthur grant); LatinX in AI (NeurIPS/ICRA/ACL workshops); Queer in AI (1,000+ Slack, ACL first archival papers 2025); AI4ALL (Dr. Fei-Fei Li co-founded, Stanford/UCSF summer programs).
