} } Our Programs. LEARN MORE #block-459a67713ae5b05a35d0 {--sqs-block-content-flex: 0; } Research Workshops at Top AI Conferences LatinX in AI hosts flagship research workshops at premier AI conferences—including NeurIPS, ICML, CVPR, and NAACL—to amplify the voices and contributions of LatinX researchers on the global stage. These workshops provide a welcoming platform for scholars at all stages to present original research, gain visibility, and engage in technical dialogue with the broader AI community.Each workshop features:Peer-reviewed research papers spanning machine learning, computer vision, NLP, ethics, and moreInvited talks from leading LatinX scientists and industry pioneersMentoring sessions, panels, and networking designed to build community and advance careersThrough these workshops, LatinX in AI has built a recognized space for collaboration, visibility, and scholarly excellence—helping hundreds of researchers develop confidence, connect with peers, and access global opportunities in AI. Mentorship That Transforms Careers LatinX in AI’s Mentorship Program connects emerging LatinX talent with experienced researchers, engineers, founders, and industry professionals across the globe. Our goal is to create pathways for growth, confidence, and opportunity in the field of AI.Through structured 1:1 mentorship, group sessions, and peer circles, we support mentees in areas such as:Graduate school applications and academic publishingCareer transitions and technical interviewsResearch feedback and professional developmentWith hundreds of matches made across borders and disciplines, our mentorship network is a cornerstone of our mission—building bridges between generations of LatinX AI leaders and cultivating a more inclusive future for artificial intelligence. Journal of LatinX in AI Research (JLXAIR) The LatinX in AI Research Journal is an open-access publication dedicated to showcasing original work from LatinX researchers across the artificial intelligence landscape. Our journal provides a platform for emerging voices, interdisciplinary ideas, and socially impactful research that often goes underrepresented in mainstream venues.Through regular issues and conference-aligned special editions, we feature:Peer-reviewed papers spanning machine learning, ethics, computer vision, NLP, and moreStudent and early-career research highlightsCollaborative works between academia, industry, and civil societyBy centering LatinX perspectives in AI, our journal not only elevates scholarship but also builds visibility, confidence, and academic credibility for contributors—especially those from developing countries and underfunded institutions. Supercomputer Access Network Through the LXAI Supercomputer Access Network, we provide LatinX researchers, students, and educators with free or low-cost access to cutting-edge AI computing infrastructure—including GPU clusters and supercomputing resources—via partnerships with academic institutions, industry donors, and national labs.This initiative addresses one of the most critical barriers in AI research: lack of access to high-performance computing. Our network enables under-resourced scholars, particularly from Latin America and other developing regions, to:Train large-scale AI modelsRun complex experimentsCollaborate on globally competitive research projectsBy democratizing access to compute, we’re leveling the playing field and fostering a new generation of LatinX innovators in artificial intelligence. LXAI Podcast: Voices of the LatinX AI Community The LatinX in AI Podcast is a storytelling platform that amplifies the voices of LatinX leaders, researchers, entrepreneurs, and changemakers in the field of artificial intelligence. Through in-depth interviews and topical discussions, we explore the intersections of technology, identity, equity, and innovation—bringing forward stories that are too often left out of the AI narrative.Each episode highlights:Breakthrough research and startup journeysInsights into ethical AI, policy, and social impactThe lived experiences of LatinX professionals navigating the tech worldOur podcast builds connection across borders and disciplines while inspiring the next generation of LatinX talent. Whether you’re in academia, industry, or just starting out, the LXAI Podcast offers valuable perspectives from across our global community. LATAM Undergraduate Research Program In collaboration with Google Explore CSR and LatinX in AI, the LATAM Undergraduate Research Program empowers underrepresented undergraduate (and master’s-level) students in computer science and related fields. Through a semester-long mentorship and project-based experience, students develop confidence, critical research skills, and a stronger sense of belonging in the AI community.What participants can expect:Hands-on research projects under the guidance of experienced mentorsOnline workshops, training, and peer activities designed to improve research methods, technical skills, and academic readinessSupport aimed at motivating students to pursue graduate studies in computing and AIThis program not only equips students with practical experience but also strengthens their identity as researchers and accelerates their academic and professional journey in AI. .fe-68c23ca7b0cea560b27e5687 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); } } 1,100+ Grants Awarded Every LatinX in AI research workshop is made possible by generous partnerships with individuals and companies committed to equity in AI. Together, we raise funds for the LXAI Travel and Registration Grant Program, which helps remove financial barriers for underrepresented scholars.Our grants are primarily awarded to students from Latin American countries and Hispanic Serving Institutions in the United States—many of whom would not be able to attend without this support. These grants help cover critical costs like travel, lodging, and conference registration, ensuring that brilliant minds from developing regions can present their work, network, and grow their careers.To date, LatinX in AI has awarded over 1,100 conference and travel grants, directly enabling the participation of LatinX researchers and students in the global AI conversation. Our Impact. 10,000+ Members Served LatinX in AI is proud to serve a vibrant, growing network of over 10,000 LatinX researchers, engineers, entrepreneurs, and students across the globe. Through our targeted programs, we are breaking down systemic barriers and expanding opportunities in the field of artificial intelligence.Our community members benefit from:- Research Workshops at top-tier AI conferences- 1:1 and group mentorship across academia and industry- Collaborative publications via our open-access research journal- Access to AI compute resources for under-resourced researchers and studentsTogether, these programs form a powerful ecosystem for professional growth, academic advancement, and cross-border collaboration—amplifying LatinX voices and talent in AI worldwide. 30+ Countries Represented LatinX in AI is a truly global community, representing all Latin-identified countries across North, Central, and South America, the Caribbean, and beyond. Our members span academic institutions, tech companies, and research labs in over 30 countries, including Brazil, Mexico, Colombia, Argentina, Peru, Chile, Venezuela, the Dominican Republic, and many others.We also proudly serve LatinX individuals across the diaspora—including those based in the United States, Canada, Spain, and the UK—who identify with and support our mission to create a more inclusive AI ecosystem.By uniting voices from across borders, languages, and cultures, LatinX in AI fosters cross-regional collaboration, shared knowledge, and a collective effort to shape the future of artificial intelligence in a way that reflects the diversity of our world. .fe-68c2358ee1f8ff7b36c5c3f0 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); We will cover the costs of travel to/from London, any visa expenses, accommodation for the full duration of the programme, and provide meals, drinks and snacks on programme days. We aim to ensure that finances do not present a barrier to anyone’s participation in ARENA.If you’re interested in applying to future iterations of ARENA but won’t be able to attend ARENA 8.0, please fill in this expression of interest form to ensure you’re kept up-to-date for when we open applications for future iterations.For more information about the programme, see our announcement post on LessWrong. #block-yui_3_17_2_1_1751882471278_3021 { --tweak-text-block-radius: 0px 0px 0px 0px; } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --tweak-text-block-blend : normal; } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --blur-value: blur(15px);} #block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { @media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } }#block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 .sqs-html-content { @media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } }@media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } How do we operate? ARENA provides in-person programming bootcamps, equipping people with the skills, community and confidence to contribute to technical AI safety. We run 2-3 bootcamps each year, and each lasts for 4-5 weeks. We’re based at the London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA) in Shoreditch, London.For those who wish to use our materials – whether to learn independently, or run their own AI safety courses – our curriculum is available online.We are grateful to be funded by Coefficient Giving. Who should apply to ARENA? There’s no single profile that we look for at ARENA; in recent iterations, successful applicants have come from diverse academic and professional backgrounds. We intend to keep it this way – this diversity makes our bootcamps a more enriching learning experience for all.When assessing applications to our programme, we like to see:Applicants who genuinely care about AI safety and making the future development of AI go well;Applicants who are able to code well in Python, and have some knowledge of the maths needed for modern AI (linear algebra, calculus, probability);A solid understanding of how you might best contribute to technical AI safety, and how you expect ARENA to help you achieve your goals.Since ARENA is an ML bootcamp, some level of technical skill in maths and coding will be required – more detail on this can be found in our FAQs. However, if our work resonates with you, we encourage you to apply. How might I benefit from ARENA? ARENA can accelerate people’s journey into impactful AI safety work by providing them with the skills, connections, and confidence required to take their next steps. Being situated at LISA also helps our participants build great relationships with people from across the AI safety community.Our alumni have gone onto:Work as AI safety engineers at Apollo Research, Anthropic and METR;Become MATS scholars and LASR participants;Start their own AI safety organisations;Pursue impactful research at world-class academic institutions.During ARENA, we run careers events with a view to helping participants with what comes next. We endeavour to keep in touch with our alumni and look after them beyond their time with us. How do I apply? Please find our applications page here.For our in-person programmes, we operate a three-stage applications process:Initial application form (this should take fewer than 90 minutes);Coding test (six questions within 1-hour time limit);Final interview: a 30-minute discussion with one of our team members exploring your interests, motivations, and technical skills. .fe-67e146e132bcbc72c7a584d6 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); } .fe-block-yui_3_17_2_1_1751882471278_2639 { --blur-value: blur(15px);} #block-yui_3_17_2_1_1751882471278_2639 { @media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } }#block-yui_3_17_2_1_1751882471278_2639 .sqs-html-content { @media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } }@media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } We are delighted to announce that our programme’s eighth iteration, ARENA 8.0, will be taking place in summer 2026!Applications to ARENA 8.0 are now closed.ARENA 8.0 will be running in-person at the London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA) from May 25th – June 26th. We will cover the costs of travel to/from London, any visa expenses, accommodation for the full duration of the programme, and provide meals, drinks and snacks on programme days. We aim to ensure that finances do not present a barrier to anyone’s participation in ARENA.If you’re interested in applying to future iterations of ARENA but won’t be able to attend ARENA 8.0, please fill in this expression of interest form to ensure you’re kept up-to-date for when we open applications for future iterations.For more information about the programme, see our announcement post on LessWrong. #block-yui_3_17_2_1_1751882471278_3021 { --tweak-text-block-radius: 0px 0px 0px 0px; } } Programme Details: #block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --tweak-text-block-radius: 0px 0px 0px 0px; --tweak-text-block-radius: unset; --stroke-style: none;--stroke-thickness: 6px; --tweak-text-block-background-color: initial; } #block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 .sqs-html-content { --tweak-text-block-padding: 6% 6% 6% 6%; --tweak-text-block-padding: initial; } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { mix-blend-mode: var(--tweak-text-block-blend ); } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --tweak-text-block-blend : normal; } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --blur-value: blur(15px);} #block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { @media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } }#block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 .sqs-html-content { @media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } }@media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } How do we operate? ARENA provides in-person programming bootcamps, equipping people with the skills, community and confidence to contribute to technical AI safety. We run 2-3 bootcamps each year, and each lasts for 4-5 weeks. We’re based at the London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA) in Shoreditch, London.For those who wish to use our materials – whether to learn independently, or run their own AI safety courses – our curriculum is available online.We are grateful to be funded by Coefficient Giving. Who should apply to ARENA? There’s no single profile that we look for at ARENA; in recent iterations, successful applicants have come from diverse academic and professional backgrounds. We intend to keep it this way – this diversity makes our bootcamps a more enriching learning experience for all.When assessing applications to our programme, we like to see:Applicants who genuinely care about AI safety and making the future development of AI go well;Applicants who are able to code well in Python, and have some knowledge of the maths needed for modern AI (linear algebra, calculus, probability);A solid understanding of how you might best contribute to technical AI safety, and how you expect ARENA to help you achieve your goals.Since ARENA is an ML bootcamp, some level of technical skill in maths and coding will be required – more detail on this can be found in our FAQs. However, if our work resonates with you, we encourage you to apply. How might I benefit from ARENA? ARENA can accelerate people’s journey into impactful AI safety work by providing them with the skills, connections, and confidence required to take their next steps. Being situated at LISA also helps our participants build great relationships with people from across the AI safety community.Our alumni have gone onto:Work as AI safety engineers at Apollo Research, Anthropic and METR;Become MATS scholars and LASR participants;Start their own AI safety organisations;Pursue impactful research at world-class academic institutions.During ARENA, we run careers events with a view to helping participants with what comes next. We endeavour to keep in touch with our alumni and look after them beyond their time with us. How do I apply? Please find our applications page here.For our in-person programmes, we operate a three-stage applications process:Initial application form (this should take fewer than 90 minutes);Coding test (six questions within 1-hour time limit);Final interview: a 30-minute discussion with one of our team members exploring your interests, motivations, and technical skills.

communities.groupName: Mox (SF) / EA Global / ARENA / AI Safety London / Stanford AI Alignment

communities.name: EA Global
communities.description: Five safety-focused communities: Mox (SF's largest AI safety space, 377 events in past year, partners MIRI/FAR.AI/Redwood); EA Global (application-based conferences in SF Feb 2026 + NYC Oct 2025, 1,000+ attendees); ARENA (4–5 week ML bootcamp London Jan–Feb 2026); AI Safety London (200 members); Stanford AI Alignment (weekly meetings with UC Berkeley/UCLA/Caltech).

We will cover the costs of travel to/from London, any visa expenses, accommodation for the full duration of the programme, and provide meals, drinks and snacks on programme days. We aim to ensure that finances do not present a barrier to anyone’s participation in ARENA.If you’re interested in applying to future iterations of ARENA but won’t be able to attend ARENA 8.0, please fill in this expression of interest form to ensure you’re kept up-to-date for when we open applications for future iterations.For more information about the programme, see our announcement post on LessWrong. #block-yui_3_17_2_1_1751882471278_3021 { --tweak-text-block-radius: 0px 0px 0px 0px; } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --tweak-text-block-blend : normal; } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --blur-value: blur(15px);} #block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { @media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } }#block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 .sqs-html-content { @media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } }@media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } How do we operate? ARENA provides in-person programming bootcamps, equipping people with the skills, community and confidence to contribute to technical AI safety. We run 2-3 bootcamps each year, and each lasts for 4-5 weeks. We’re based at the London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA) in Shoreditch, London.For those who wish to use our materials – whether to learn independently, or run their own AI safety courses – our curriculum is available online.We are grateful to be funded by Coefficient Giving. Who should apply to ARENA? There’s no single profile that we look for at ARENA; in recent iterations, successful applicants have come from diverse academic and professional backgrounds. We intend to keep it this way – this diversity makes our bootcamps a more enriching learning experience for all.When assessing applications to our programme, we like to see:Applicants who genuinely care about AI safety and making the future development of AI go well;Applicants who are able to code well in Python, and have some knowledge of the maths needed for modern AI (linear algebra, calculus, probability);A solid understanding of how you might best contribute to technical AI safety, and how you expect ARENA to help you achieve your goals.Since ARENA is an ML bootcamp, some level of technical skill in maths and coding will be required – more detail on this can be found in our FAQs. However, if our work resonates with you, we encourage you to apply. How might I benefit from ARENA? ARENA can accelerate people’s journey into impactful AI safety work by providing them with the skills, connections, and confidence required to take their next steps. Being situated at LISA also helps our participants build great relationships with people from across the AI safety community.Our alumni have gone onto:Work as AI safety engineers at Apollo Research, Anthropic and METR;Become MATS scholars and LASR participants;Start their own AI safety organisations;Pursue impactful research at world-class academic institutions.During ARENA, we run careers events with a view to helping participants with what comes next. We endeavour to keep in touch with our alumni and look after them beyond their time with us. How do I apply? Please find our applications page here.For our in-person programmes, we operate a three-stage applications process:Initial application form (this should take fewer than 90 minutes);Coding test (six questions within 1-hour time limit);Final interview: a 30-minute discussion with one of our team members exploring your interests, motivations, and technical skills. .fe-67e146e132bcbc72c7a584d6 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); We will cover the costs of travel to/from London, any visa expenses, accommodation for the full duration of the programme, and provide meals, drinks and snacks on programme days. We aim to ensure that finances do not present a barrier to anyone’s participation in ARENA.If you’re interested in applying to future iterations of ARENA but won’t be able to attend ARENA 8.0, please fill in this expression of interest form to ensure you’re kept up-to-date for when we open applications for future iterations.For more information about the programme, see our announcement post on LessWrong. #block-yui_3_17_2_1_1751882471278_3021 { --tweak-text-block-radius: 0px 0px 0px 0px; } } Programme Details: #block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --tweak-text-block-radius: 0px 0px 0px 0px; --tweak-text-block-radius: unset; --stroke-style: none;--stroke-thickness: 6px; --tweak-text-block-background-color: initial; } #block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 .sqs-html-content { --tweak-text-block-padding: 6% 6% 6% 6%; --tweak-text-block-padding: initial; } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { mix-blend-mode: var(--tweak-text-block-blend ); } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --tweak-text-block-blend : normal; } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --blur-value: blur(15px);} #block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { @media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } }#block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 .sqs-html-content { @media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } }@media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } How do we operate? ARENA provides in-person programming bootcamps, equipping people with the skills, community and confidence to contribute to technical AI safety. We run 2-3 bootcamps each year, and each lasts for 4-5 weeks. We’re based at the London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA) in Shoreditch, London.For those who wish to use our materials – whether to learn independently, or run their own AI safety courses – our curriculum is available online.We are grateful to be funded by Coefficient Giving. Who should apply to ARENA? There’s no single profile that we look for at ARENA; in recent iterations, successful applicants have come from diverse academic and professional backgrounds. We intend to keep it this way – this diversity makes our bootcamps a more enriching learning experience for all.When assessing applications to our programme, we like to see:Applicants who genuinely care about AI safety and making the future development of AI go well;Applicants who are able to code well in Python, and have some knowledge of the maths needed for modern AI (linear algebra, calculus, probability);A solid understanding of how you might best contribute to technical AI safety, and how you expect ARENA to help you achieve your goals.Since ARENA is an ML bootcamp, some level of technical skill in maths and coding will be required – more detail on this can be found in our FAQs. However, if our work resonates with you, we encourage you to apply. How might I benefit from ARENA? ARENA can accelerate people’s journey into impactful AI safety work by providing them with the skills, connections, and confidence required to take their next steps. Being situated at LISA also helps our participants build great relationships with people from across the AI safety community.Our alumni have gone onto:Work as AI safety engineers at Apollo Research, Anthropic and METR;Become MATS scholars and LASR participants;Start their own AI safety organisations;Pursue impactful research at world-class academic institutions.During ARENA, we run careers events with a view to helping participants with what comes next. We endeavour to keep in touch with our alumni and look after them beyond their time with us. How do I apply? Please find our applications page here.For our in-person programmes, we operate a three-stage applications process:Initial application form (this should take fewer than 90 minutes);Coding test (six questions within 1-hour time limit);Final interview: a 30-minute discussion with one of our team members exploring your interests, motivations, and technical skills.

communities.groupName: Mox (SF) / EA Global / ARENA / AI Safety London / Stanford AI Alignment

communities.name: ARENA
communities.description: Five safety-focused communities: Mox (SF's largest AI safety space, 377 events in past year, partners MIRI/FAR.AI/Redwood); EA Global (application-based conferences in SF Feb 2026 + NYC Oct 2025, 1,000+ attendees); ARENA (4–5 week ML bootcamp London Jan–Feb 2026); AI Safety London (200 members); Stanford AI Alignment (weekly meetings with UC Berkeley/UCLA/Caltech).

We will cover the costs of travel to/from London, any visa expenses, accommodation for the full duration of the programme, and provide meals, drinks and snacks on programme days. We aim to ensure that finances do not present a barrier to anyone’s participation in ARENA.If you’re interested in applying to future iterations of ARENA but won’t be able to attend ARENA 8.0, please fill in this expression of interest form to ensure you’re kept up-to-date for when we open applications for future iterations.For more information about the programme, see our announcement post on LessWrong. #block-yui_3_17_2_1_1751882471278_3021 { --tweak-text-block-radius: 0px 0px 0px 0px; } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --tweak-text-block-blend : normal; } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --blur-value: blur(15px);} #block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { @media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } }#block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 .sqs-html-content { @media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } }@media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } How do we operate? ARENA provides in-person programming bootcamps, equipping people with the skills, community and confidence to contribute to technical AI safety. We run 2-3 bootcamps each year, and each lasts for 4-5 weeks. We’re based at the London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA) in Shoreditch, London.For those who wish to use our materials – whether to learn independently, or run their own AI safety courses – our curriculum is available online.We are grateful to be funded by Coefficient Giving. Who should apply to ARENA? There’s no single profile that we look for at ARENA; in recent iterations, successful applicants have come from diverse academic and professional backgrounds. We intend to keep it this way – this diversity makes our bootcamps a more enriching learning experience for all.When assessing applications to our programme, we like to see:Applicants who genuinely care about AI safety and making the future development of AI go well;Applicants who are able to code well in Python, and have some knowledge of the maths needed for modern AI (linear algebra, calculus, probability);A solid understanding of how you might best contribute to technical AI safety, and how you expect ARENA to help you achieve your goals.Since ARENA is an ML bootcamp, some level of technical skill in maths and coding will be required – more detail on this can be found in our FAQs. However, if our work resonates with you, we encourage you to apply. How might I benefit from ARENA? ARENA can accelerate people’s journey into impactful AI safety work by providing them with the skills, connections, and confidence required to take their next steps. Being situated at LISA also helps our participants build great relationships with people from across the AI safety community.Our alumni have gone onto:Work as AI safety engineers at Apollo Research, Anthropic and METR;Become MATS scholars and LASR participants;Start their own AI safety organisations;Pursue impactful research at world-class academic institutions.During ARENA, we run careers events with a view to helping participants with what comes next. We endeavour to keep in touch with our alumni and look after them beyond their time with us. How do I apply? Please find our applications page here.For our in-person programmes, we operate a three-stage applications process:Initial application form (this should take fewer than 90 minutes);Coding test (six questions within 1-hour time limit);Final interview: a 30-minute discussion with one of our team members exploring your interests, motivations, and technical skills. .fe-67e146e132bcbc72c7a584d6 { --grid-gutter: calc(var(--sqs-mobile-site-gutter, 6vw) - 11.0px); We will cover the costs of travel to/from London, any visa expenses, accommodation for the full duration of the programme, and provide meals, drinks and snacks on programme days. We aim to ensure that finances do not present a barrier to anyone’s participation in ARENA.If you’re interested in applying to future iterations of ARENA but won’t be able to attend ARENA 8.0, please fill in this expression of interest form to ensure you’re kept up-to-date for when we open applications for future iterations.For more information about the programme, see our announcement post on LessWrong. #block-yui_3_17_2_1_1751882471278_3021 { --tweak-text-block-radius: 0px 0px 0px 0px; } } Programme Details: #block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --tweak-text-block-radius: 0px 0px 0px 0px; --tweak-text-block-radius: unset; --stroke-style: none;--stroke-thickness: 6px; --tweak-text-block-background-color: initial; } #block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 .sqs-html-content { --tweak-text-block-padding: 6% 6% 6% 6%; --tweak-text-block-padding: initial; } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { mix-blend-mode: var(--tweak-text-block-blend ); } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --tweak-text-block-blend : normal; } .fe-block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { --blur-value: blur(15px);} #block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 { @media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } }#block-8bb47a890777c8143ff1 .sqs-html-content { @media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } }@media screen and (max-width: 767px) { } How do we operate? ARENA provides in-person programming bootcamps, equipping people with the skills, community and confidence to contribute to technical AI safety. We run 2-3 bootcamps each year, and each lasts for 4-5 weeks. We’re based at the London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA) in Shoreditch, London.For those who wish to use our materials – whether to learn independently, or run their own AI safety courses – our curriculum is available online.We are grateful to be funded by Coefficient Giving. Who should apply to ARENA? There’s no single profile that we look for at ARENA; in recent iterations, successful applicants have come from diverse academic and professional backgrounds. We intend to keep it this way – this diversity makes our bootcamps a more enriching learning experience for all.When assessing applications to our programme, we like to see:Applicants who genuinely care about AI safety and making the future development of AI go well;Applicants who are able to code well in Python, and have some knowledge of the maths needed for modern AI (linear algebra, calculus, probability);A solid understanding of how you might best contribute to technical AI safety, and how you expect ARENA to help you achieve your goals.Since ARENA is an ML bootcamp, some level of technical skill in maths and coding will be required – more detail on this can be found in our FAQs. However, if our work resonates with you, we encourage you to apply. How might I benefit from ARENA? ARENA can accelerate people’s journey into impactful AI safety work by providing them with the skills, connections, and confidence required to take their next steps. Being situated at LISA also helps our participants build great relationships with people from across the AI safety community.Our alumni have gone onto:Work as AI safety engineers at Apollo Research, Anthropic and METR;Become MATS scholars and LASR participants;Start their own AI safety organisations;Pursue impactful research at world-class academic institutions.During ARENA, we run careers events with a view to helping participants with what comes next. We endeavour to keep in touch with our alumni and look after them beyond their time with us. How do I apply? Please find our applications page here.For our in-person programmes, we operate a three-stage applications process:Initial application form (this should take fewer than 90 minutes);Coding test (six questions within 1-hour time limit);Final interview: a 30-minute discussion with one of our team members exploring your interests, motivations, and technical skills.
